channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Panel looking into Trump assassination attempt says Secret Service needs ‘fundamental reform’
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An independent panel investigating the attempted assassination of Donald Trump at a Pennsylvania campaign rally says the Secret Services needs fundamental reform" and that “another Butler can and will happen again” without major changes in how candidates are protected.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Death of ex-One Direction member Liam Payne at 31 shocks fans around the world
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The death of Liam Payne, who shot to stardom as a member of the boy band One Direction and grappled with intense global fame while still in his teens, sent shockwaves across the world Thursday as Argentine authorities continued their investigation at the scene.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Hell's Angels, Red Devils motorcycle gang members charged in NC
item.description: Sixteen members of the Hell's Angels and Red Devils motorcycle gangs are charged in an eight-count federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Baron James, NewsNation

item.title: Funeral home owner pleads guilty to assaulting police and journalists during Capitol riot
item.description: A Long Island funeral home owner has pleaded guilty to spraying wasp killer at police officers and assaulting two journalists, including an Associated Press photographer, during a mob’s riot at the U.S. Capitol nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar may be dead
item.description: The Israeli military said Thursday it was looking into whether Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in a military operation in Gaza. Authorities were conducting DNA tests on a body to determine if it is him, an Israeli security official said.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, KREX 5

item.title: Michigan teen facing charges in sextortion case: Deputies
item.description: Deputies say a Michigan teen is facing charges after allegedly pressuring other teenagers to send inappropriate images.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: Israel is DNA-testing a body from Gaza to see if it's Hamas' top leader Sinwar, an official says
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Israeli military said Thursday it was looking into whether Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in a military operation in Gaza. Authorities were conducting DNA tests on a body to determine if it is him, an Israeli security official said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of LA to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Person whose DNA was found on collar of dead puppy tied to fire hydrant has died
item.description: After a pit bull puppy was found dead, tied to a fire hydrant in Richmond's East End in November of 2023, investigators have released new information and closed the case.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Adult day centers offer multicultural hubs for older people of color
item.description: Adult day centers are the most racially diverse long-term care setting in the U.S. They also serve the least amount of people of all long-term care settings, in part because of the cost and limited insurance coverage options.
item.dc:creator: Devi Shastri And Laura Bargfeld, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After hurricane, with no running water, residents organize to meet a basic need
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — It takes water to flush a toilet and tens of thousands of North Carolinians have been without it since Hurricane Helene ripped through the state three weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 dead, at least 4 injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and at least four were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, Fox 5

item.title: The Biden administration has now canceled loans for more than 1 million public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama to execute man for killing 5 in what he says was a meth-fueled rampage
item.description: Alabama prepared Thursday to put to death a man who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals to allow his execution to go forward. Derrick Dearman, 36, is scheduled to be executed by lethal injection at 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene: 'Backbone of America' helping farmers across Southeast who lost billions in crops, land
item.description: Hurricane Helene has devastated farmland and killed livestock across the Southeast. Experts estimate agricultural damages from the hurricane could be billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Navy jet located after crashing near Mount Rainier in Washington
item.description: The U.S. Navy located an EA-18G Growler jet on Wednesday that crashed near Mount Rainier in Washington on Tuesday, though the status of the two-person crew is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Harris holds 2-point edge in tight North Carolina race: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a 2-point lead over former President Trump among likely voters in North Carolina, according to a poll released Wednesday. The Quinnipiac University poll found the vice president garnering the support of 49 percent of likely voters to the former president’s 47 percent.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Google Trends 'Frightgeist' lists the most popular Halloween costumes for 2024
item.description: Whether you are unsure about what your Halloween costume should be or want to stand out from the crowd, Google has you covered.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: 2 visitors to Hawaii dead after being swept out to sea
item.description: Safety officials said there were signs posted for beachgoers, warning of the strong current and dangerous shore break.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Florino, NewsNation

item.title: People with felony records can now vote in Nebraska — and it could help tip the balance in November
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Nebraska’s top election official had no authority to strip voting rights from people convicted of a felony, the state Supreme Court ruled Wednesday in a decision that could add hundreds of new voters to the rolls and potentially help tip the balance on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia residents complain of squatters in home owned by realty agency: 'It's a problem'
item.description: Angry residents at a housing complex in an Atlanta suburb said a handful of squatters have taken over a home in the area, leading to HOA rule violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: The Nebraska Supreme Court rejected the contention that the state’s pardon board has sole authority to restore such rights.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA leader: Misinformation about hurricane operations must stop
item.description: "The ongoing spread of misinformation about operations is not OK," FEMA Administrator Deanne Criswell said.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Family of Menendez brothers demands their release from prison
item.description: The family of Lyle and Erik Menendez, as well as their attorney, held a press conference Wednesday to discuss prosecutors’ review of their convictions for murdering their parents, José and Kitty Menendez, in Beverly Hills in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After 36 years, accused killer in Oregon cold case to stand trial
item.description: An autopsy revealed Deborah Atrops was physically assaulted and strangled to death. She was 30. Then, the case went cold.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The son of a South Carolina inmate urges the governor to save his father from execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Richard Moore never meant to kill anyone the night he robbed a South Carolina convenience store and the Black man was convicted by a jury with no African Americans, his son and lawyers say as they fight to save the inmate from execution next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls for protester to 'go back home to Mommy' during California rally
item.description: Trump called for the protester to “get the hell knocked out of her,” his latest instance of using violent language when confronted by demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
